message.content:
Репозиторий: /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP (монорепо: server/ Node+TS+Express+Mongoose+OpenAI; client/ React+Vite — это админка).
Ревью изменений ветки feat/knowledge-bank поверх базы 6431bf3..HEAD (2 коммита: админ-UI банка + переменный размер теста и качество вопросов).
Сначала прочитай диапазон диффа:  git -C /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP diff 6431bf3 HEAD -- <относящиеся пути>
Затем читай окружающий код, чтобы понять контекст. Ищи РЕАЛЬНЫЕ дефекты корректности и интеграции, а не стиль.
Контекст фич:
 - Бэкенд KC/банк (questionBank.service.ts, ktp.controller.ts, ktp.routes.ts): новый read-эндпоинт listItems вопросов банка (admin); size-параметр assembleNodeTest (разрешено 5/10/15/20), size подмешан в реюз-хэш; generateForCoverage переписан на циклы раундов + разброс difficulty; contentHashOf теперь хеширует текст+варианты+правильный ответ.
 - Test.model.ts: валидатор questions.length сменён с ===10 на диапазон 1..120.
 - test.routes.ts/test.controller.ts: /tests/node-bank принимает size; /generate(+guest) валидируют questionCount.
 - client: ktp.api.ts (+методы KC/банка), ktp.types.ts (+типы), components/ktp/TopicKnowledgeBank.tsx (новый), KtpCatalogPage.tsx (врезка per-topic), гейтинг по role==='admin'.
Возвращай только обоснованные находки с file/location/severity/detail/suggestedFix.

Адверсариально ПРОВЕРЬ находку — попробуй её ОПРОВЕРГНУТЬ, прочитав реальный код. Если это не настоящий дефект (ложноположительное, или уже корректно обрабатывается), верни isReal=false. По умолчанию при сомнении — isReal=false.

Находка:
{
  "file": "server/src/services/questionBank.service.ts",
  "location": "contentHashOf (lines ~32-47) — смена сигнатуры string→IQuestion и формулы хэша",
  "severity": "medium",
  "title": "Смена формулы contentHash несовместима с уже сохранёнными item'ами (дедуп против старых записей ломается)",
  "detail": "contentHashOf раньше хэшировал только нормализованный questionText, теперь — текст+отсортированные options+correctOption. Уникальный индекс {subjectId,knowledgeNodeId,contentHash} (QuestionItem.model.ts:89) опирается на это значение. Хэш пересчитывается ТОЛЬКО при вставке нового item'а; ранее сохранённые записи сохраняют старый text-only хэш и не мигрируются. Следствие: при дозаполнении банка на узле, где уже есть старые item'ы, новый вопрос с тем же текстом (но это перефразировка с теми же опциями или наоборот) посчитается по новой формуле и не столкнётся со старой записью по индексу — появятся фактические дубли/почти-дубли, которые раньше отсекались. Это деградация инварианта дедупа на смешанных (старых+новых) данных, а не на чистой базе.",
  "suggestedFix": "Либо одноразовая миграция: пересчитать contentHash всех существующих QuestionItem по новой формуле (с обработкой возможных коллизий уникального индекса), либо версионировать хэш (префикс схемы v2) и осознанно принять, что дедуп работает только внутри одной версии. Зафиксировать решение в коде/миграции, иначе старый банк копит дубли."
}

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- figma:figma-code-connect: Creates and maintains Figma Code Connect template files that map Figma components to code snippets. Use when the user mentions Code Connect, Figma component mapping, design-to-code translation, or asks to create/update .figma.ts or .figma.js files.
- figma:figma-create-new-file: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `create_new_file` tool call. NEVER call `create_new_file` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user wants a new blank Figma file — a new design, FigJam, or Slides file — or when you need a fresh file before calling `use_figma`. Usage — /figma-create-new-file [editorType] [fileName] (e.g. /figma-create-new-file figjam My Whiteboard, /figma-create-new-file slides Q3 Review)
- figma:figma-generate-design: Use this skill alongside figma-use when the task involves translating an application page, view, or multi-section layout into Figma. Triggers: 'write to Figma', 'create in Figma from code', 'push page to Figma', 'take this app/page and build it in Figma', 'create a screen', 'build a landing page in Figma', 'update the Figma screen to match code', 'convert this modal/dialog/drawer/panel to Figma'. This is the preferred workflow skill whenever the user wants to build or update a full page, modal, dialog, drawer, sidebar, panel, or any composed multi-section view in Figma from code or a description. Discovers design system components, variables, and styles from Code Connect files, existing screens, and library search, then imports them and assembles views incrementally section-by-section using design system tokens instead of hardcoded values.
- figma:figma-generate-diagram: MANDATORY prerequisite — load this skill BEFORE every `generate_diagram` tool call. NEVER call `generate_diagram` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user asks to create, generate, draw, render, sketch, or build a diagram — flowchart, architecture diagram, sequence diagram, ERD or entity-relationship diagram, state diagram or state machine, gantt chart, or timeline. Also trigger when the user mentions Mermaid syntax or wants a system architecture, decision tree, dependency graph, API call flow, auth handshake, schema, or pipeline visualized in FigJam. Routes to type-specific guidance, sets universal Mermaid constraints, and tells you when to use a different diagram type or skip the tool entirely (mindmaps, pie charts, class diagrams, etc.).
- figma:figma-generate-library: Build or update a professional-grade design system in Figma from a codebase. Use when the user wants to create variables/tokens, build component libraries, create individual components with proper variant sets and variable bindings, set up theming (light/dark modes), document foundations, or reconcile gaps between code and Figma. Also use when the user asks to create or generate any component in Figma — even a single one — since components require proper variable foundations, variant states, and design token bindings to be production-quality. This skill teaches WHAT to build and in WHAT ORDER — it complements the `figma-use` skill which teaches HOW to call the Plugin API. Both skills should be loaded together.
- figma:figma-swiftui: SwiftUI ↔ Figma translation. Use whenever the user mentions Swift, SwiftUI, iOS, iPhone, or iPad — in EITHER direction — translating a Figma design into SwiftUI (design → code), or pushing SwiftUI views / screens / tokens back into a Figma file (code → design). Triggers on phrases like 'implement this Figma design in SwiftUI', 'build this screen in Swift', 'push this SwiftUI view to Figma', 'mirror my Swift code in a Figma file', or whenever a Figma URL appears alongside `.swift` files / an `.xcodeproj`. Routes to a direction-specific reference doc; loads alongside `figma-use` for the code → design path.
- figma:figma-use: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `use_figma` tool call. NEVER call `use_figma` directly without loading this skill first. Skipping it causes common, hard-to-debug failures. Trigger whenever the user wants to perform a write action or a unique read action that requires JavaScript execution in the Figma file context — e.g. create/edit/delete nodes, set up variables or tokens, build components and variants, modify auto-layout or fills, bind variables to properties, or inspect file structure programmatically.
- figma:figma-use-figjam: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the FigJam context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- figma:figma-use-slides: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the Slides context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify this finding by examining the actual code changes to `contentHashOf` and understanding the deduplication mechanism.
I have the full diff. Now let me verify the key claims in the finding by examining the QuestionItem model (the unique index) and checking whether there's any migration or hash versioning mechanism.

content.text:
Let me verify the facts of the analysis. The finding's core claim is correct on the mechanics. But I need to adversarially assess whether this constitutes a *real defect* versus an intended/benign behavior change. Two key questions:

1. Does the `contentHash` formula change actually cause a *correctness* problem (dedup failure) on mixed data?
2. Is there a mitigating factor — e.g., the `BANK_PROMPT_VERSION` bump to `bank-v2`, or the fact that the dedup is best-effort?

Let me check the types and how dedup interacts, plus whether old data realistically exists (smoke/scan scripts give clues about deployment maturity).

Now I have enough context to adversarially assess this finding. Let me reason carefully.

**The mechanical claims are factually accurate:**
- `contentHashOf` changed from `(questionText: string)` hashing only normalized text to `(q: IQuestion)` hashing text + sorted options + correctOption.
- The unique index `{subjectId, knowledgeNodeId, contentHash}` exists (QuestionItem.model.ts:89).
- Hash is computed only at insert time; no migration recomputes existing rows.

**Now the adversarial critique — is this a REAL defect?**

The finding's claimed consequence is internally contradictory and overstated. Let me reason about what the hash change actually does to dedup behavior on mixed (old+new) data:

1. **Old hash = sha256(text)**. **New hash = sha256(text + options + correct)**. The new hash is a *superset* of the inputs. The text is still the dominant component (it's always included verbatim).

2. **For a true duplicate** (same text, same options, same correct answer — which is the *only* thing an LLM regenerating the identical question would produce), the old row's stored hash is `H_old = sha256(text)` and the new row's computed hash is `H_new = sha256(text|opts|correct)`. These differ, so the unique index does NOT collide → the duplicate *would* be inserted.

So the finding is correct that **exact duplicates against pre-existing old rows are no longer caught by the index**. That part is real.

3. **However**, the finding's narrative example is self-contradictory: "новый вопрос с тем же текстом (но это перефразировка с теми же опциями или наоборот)". A rephrasing has *different* text, so it never collided under the old text-only hash either. The new formula doesn't make rephrasing-dedup worse — neither old nor new hash catches rephrasings (both are exact-match hashes, not semantic). The only case affected is *byte-identical text*, where the old formula was actually *stricter*.

**Now, the critical mitigating factor I need to weigh:** This is a transient deduplication optimization, not a correctness invariant. Even with a fully consistent hash scheme, the dedup is best-effort:

Let me check whether this is pre-production (no real data to migrate), which determines severity. The `BANK_PROMPT_VERSION` bumped v1→v2, smoke/scan scripts exist, the whole feature is described as "Фаза 1-3" / MVP. Let me check git history depth on this file and whether QuestionItem data is plausibly deployed.

input.command: git log --oneline -15 -- server/src/services/questionBank.service.ts server/src/models/QuestionItem.model.ts 2>/dev/null; echo "---first appearance of QuestionItem model---"; git log --oneline --diff-filter=A -- server/src/models/QuestionItem.model.ts 2>/dev/null

content.text:
This confirms the picture. The entire question bank feature — including the `QuestionItem` model with its `contentHash` field and unique index — was introduced very recently in commit `473e791` (just 3 commits before the base `6431bf3`, and 5 commits before HEAD on this same `feat/knowledge-bank` branch). This is a brand-new, in-development MVP feature ("Фазы 0-3").

Let me now finalize the adversarial assessment.

**Assessment of the finding:**

The finding's *mechanical premise* is accurate (hash formula changed, no migration, index relies on the value). But several factors substantially weaken it into a false positive at the claimed "medium" severity:

1. **Self-contradictory consequence claim.** The detail says a "перефразировка с теми же опциями" (rephrasing with same options) would now slip through. This is wrong: a rephrasing has different `questionText`, so it never matched under the old text-only hash *either*. Both formulas are exact-match SHA hashes, not semantic dedup. The new formula does not regress rephrasing-dedup at all. The only case where behavior differs is *byte-identical question text* — and for those, the new formula is strictly *more permissive* against pre-existing rows only because the stored old hash was computed differently.

2. **No real data to migrate.** The `QuestionItem` collection, its `contentHash`, and the unique index were all introduced in commit `473e791`, which is the same in-development `feat/knowledge-bank` branch, only 3 commits before the review base. This is an unreleased MVP ("Фазы 0-3", smoke-test scripts, no migration framework anywhere in the repo). There is essentially no production corpus of "old text-only-hash" items to be stranded. The "mixed old+new data" scenario the finding depends on does not realistically exist.

3. **Dedup here is explicitly best-effort, not an invariant.** The insert path catches code `11000` and "молча пропускаем" (silently skips). The index is a convenience guard against re-inserting the exact same generated question, not a correctness-critical uniqueness constraint. Worst-case impact of a missed dedup is one extra near-identical bank item, which the adaptive selection and verifier tolerate. No correctness or integration behavior breaks.

4. **The intended direction is an improvement.** Adding options+correctOption to the hash *increases* dedup precision going forward (catches same-text/different-answer collisions the old hash would have wrongly merged). The transitional inconsistency is bounded to pre-existing rows on the same dev branch.

This does not meet the bar for a real defect to flag. At most it is a minor, self-resolving transitional note on a pre-release feature, and the finding's stated mechanism (rephrasing dedup regression) is incorrect. Per the "when in doubt, isReal=false" guidance, this is a false positive.

input.reason:
The mechanical premise is accurate but the finding does not rise to a real defect, and its core stated consequence is wrong.

Verified facts (server/src/services/questionBank.service.ts:33, :239; server/src/models/QuestionItem.model.ts:89): contentHashOf changed from sha256(normalized questionText) to sha256(text | sorted options | correctOption); the unique index {subjectId, knowledgeNodeId, contentHash} relies on this value; the hash is computed only at insert time and no migration recomputes existing rows. No migration framework exists anywhere in server/.

Why it is a false positive:

1) The finding's described regression is self-contradictory. It claims a "перефразировка с теми же опциями" (rephrasing with the same options) would now slip past dedup. A rephrasing has different questionText, so it never collided under the old text-only hash either — both old and new formulas are exact-match SHA hashes, not semantic dedup. The new formula does NOT worsen rephrasing dedup. The only behavior that actually differs is byte-identical question text against a pre-existing old-hash row. So the stated mechanism is incorrect.

2) No real data to strand. The entire QuestionItem collection, the contentHash field, and the unique index were all introduced in commit 473e791 ("Фазы 0-3"), only 3 commits before the review base 6431bf3, on this same in-development feat/knowledge-bank branch. This is an unreleased MVP (smoke-test scripts, no migrations). The "mixed old+new data" corpus the finding depends on does not realistically exist; on a clean base the new formula is consistent.

3) The dedup is explicitly best-effort, not a correctness invariant. The insert path catches duplicate-key error 11000 and silently skips ("молча пропускаем"); the worst case of a missed dedup is one extra near-identical bank item, which the verifier and adaptive selection tolerate. No correctness or integration behavior breaks.

4) The change is a net improvement going forward (it also catches same-text/different-answer collisions the old hash wrongly merged); any transitional inconsistency is bounded to pre-existing rows on the same dev branch and self-resolves as the bank refills.

Given the incorrect stated mechanism, the absence of real legacy data, and the best-effort nature of the index, this is below the bar to flag.

content.text:
I've completed the adversarial verification of this finding.

**Verdict: isReal = false (false positive)**

The finding's mechanical premise is accurate — `contentHashOf` did change from hashing only `questionText` to hashing text + sorted options + correctOption (questionBank.service.ts:33, :239), the unique index `{subjectId, knowledgeNodeId, contentHash}` relies on it (QuestionItem.model.ts:89), the hash is computed only at insert time, and there's no migration anywhere in the repo.

But it does not constitute a real defect, for these reasons:

1. **The stated consequence is wrong.** The finding claims a "rephrasing with the same options" would now slip past dedup. A rephrasing has different `questionText`, so it never collided under the old text-only hash either — both formulas are exact-match SHA hashes, not semantic dedup. The new formula does not regress rephrasing dedup; the only differing case is byte-identical text against a pre-existing row.

2. **No real legacy data exists.** The entire `QuestionItem` model, the `contentHash` field, and the unique index were introduced in commit `473e791` ("Фазы 0-3"), only 3 commits before the review base, on this same in-development `feat/knowledge-bank` branch. This is an unreleased MVP with smoke-test scripts and no migration framework. The "mixed old+new data" scenario the finding depends on isn't realistic; on a clean base the new formula is fully consistent.

3. **The dedup is best-effort, not an invariant.** The insert path catches the `11000` duplicate-key error and silently skips. Worst case of a missed dedup is one extra near-identical bank item, tolerated by the verifier and adaptive selection — no correctness or integration behavior breaks. The change is actually a net improvement going forward (it now catches same-text/different-answer collisions the old hash wrongly merged).
